highlights.title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 Announced
highlights.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Fable 5, a new Mythos-class model that surpasses all previously generally available models in capability. Claude Code v2.1.170 and later include access to Fable 5, so make sure you update to take advantage of its enhanced performance in your coding workflows.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.172: Sub-Agents Can Now Spawn Sub-Agents (Up to 5 Levels Deep)
highlights.summary: Claude Code now supports nested sub-agent hierarchies up to 5 levels deep, enabling more complex and autonomous multi-step task delegation. This release also improves Amazon Bedrock support by automatically reading the AWS region from `~/.aws` config files, and adds a search bar when browsing markdowns ��� useful for larger projects.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.175: enforceAvailableModels Managed Setting
highlights.summary: Enterprise and team admins can now use the new `enforceAvailableModels` setting to ensure the Default model always resolves to an approved model from the allowlist, preventing users or project configs from bypassing restrictions. This is a key governance feature for organizations deploying Claude Code at scale.

highlights.title: Running Claude Code Offline on an M3 Pro with Qwen3.6
highlights.summary: This guide walks through setting up Claude Code in a fully air-gapped environment on an Apple M3 Pro using the Qwen3.6 local model, useful for teams with strict data-security requirements or no internet access. It covers the necessary configuration steps to point Claude Code at a local model endpoint instead of Anthropic's API.

highlights.title: Show HN: macOS Menu Bar Gauges for Your Claude Code Quota
highlights.summary: This open-source macOS tool adds a live menu bar indicator showing your Claude Code API quota usage at a glance, so you never get surprised by hitting limits mid-session. It's a quick install for Mac users who want to keep an eye on consumption without interrupting their workflow.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This concise YouTube tutorial covers Claude Code from first setup all the way through advanced workflows in just 20 minutes, making it ideal for developers who want a fast but thorough introduction. If you're new to Claude Code or want a quick refresher on best practices, this is one of the most time-efficient options available.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute YouTube walkthrough designed to take developers from basic usage to more confident, productive Claude Code sessions. It's a great resource to bookmark for onboarding teammates who need to get up to speed quickly without committing to a multi-hour course.

highlights.title: Yserver: Modern X11 Server Written in Rust with the Help of Claude Code
highlights.summary: A developer used Claude Code as a core part of building Yserver, a modern Rust-based X11 server, demonstrating how Claude Code can assist with low-level systems programming tasks. This is an inspiring real-world example of Claude Code being applied beyond typical web or scripting workflows.

tips_of_the_day: If you're on a Max, Team, or Enterprise plan, open the /model picker after updating to v2.1.174 or later — Opus and Sonnet now appear as distinct rows, making it easier to consciously choose the right model for heavy vs. lightweight tasks.

Before starting a long agentic session, check your Claude Code quota using a menu bar tool or the /status command — hitting a limit mid-task can interrupt multi-step sub-agent workflows that are hard to resume cleanly.

When delegating complex tasks to sub-agents, break your goal into clearly scoped sub-tasks first; now that sub-agents can spawn their own sub-agents up to 5 levels deep (v2.1.172), well-structured prompts prevent runaway nesting and keep costs predictable.
